POMDPPlanners

Contents:

  • API Reference
POMDPPlanners
  • <no title>
  • View page source

Tests for belief implementations.

This module tests the belief implementations, focusing on: - Basic belief functionality - Belief updates - Belief sampling - Belief types

POMDPPlanners.tests.test_core.test_belief.create_belief(env, config)[source]

Create a belief from configuration.

POMDPPlanners.tests.test_core.test_belief.test_belief_config_id_different_values_produces_different_hash()[source]

Purpose: Ensures belief objects with different values generate unique config_id hashes

Given: Two TestBelief instances with different values (42 and 43) When: Config IDs are computed for both belief objects Then: The beliefs produce different config_id values to prevent cache collisions

Test type: unit

POMDPPlanners.tests.test_core.test_belief.test_belief_config_id_identical_values_produces_same_hash()[source]

Purpose: Validates that identical belief objects produce the same config_id hash for caching

Given: Two TestBelief instances initialized with identical values (42) When: Config IDs are generated for both belief objects Then: Both beliefs produce identical config_id values for proper cache functionality

Test type: unit

POMDPPlanners.tests.test_core.test_belief.test_belief_config_id_numpy_arrays_handles_identical_content()[source]

Purpose: Verifies config_id generation works correctly with numpy array belief values

Given: Two TestBelief instances containing identical numpy arrays [1, 2, 3] When: Config IDs are generated for both numpy-based beliefs Then: Both beliefs produce identical config_id values despite being separate array objects

Test type: unit

POMDPPlanners.tests.test_core.test_belief.test_belief_equality()[source]

Test belief equality.

POMDPPlanners.tests.test_core.test_belief.test_belief_equality_with_different_types()[source]

Test equality comparison with different types.

POMDPPlanners.tests.test_core.test_belief.test_belief_objects_usable_as_dictionary_keys_and_set_members()[source]

Purpose: Validates belief objects can be used as dictionary keys and set members for caching

Given: Two identical TestBelief instances with same value (42) When: Beliefs are used in sets and as dictionary keys Then: Set deduplicates identical beliefs and dictionary access works with both objects

Test type: unit

POMDPPlanners.tests.test_core.test_belief.test_belief_update_basic()[source]

Test basic belief update functionality.

POMDPPlanners.tests.test_core.test_belief.test_belief_update_consistency()[source]

Test that belief update produces consistent results.

POMDPPlanners.tests.test_core.test_belief.test_belief_update_observation_probabilities()[source]

Test that belief update correctly computes observation probabilities.

POMDPPlanners.tests.test_core.test_belief.test_belief_update_preserves_particle_count()[source]

Test that belief update preserves the number of particles.

POMDPPlanners.tests.test_core.test_belief.test_belief_update_state_transitions()[source]

Test that belief update correctly handles state transitions.

POMDPPlanners.tests.test_core.test_belief.test_belief_update_weight_normalization()[source]

Test that belief update properly handles weight normalization.

POMDPPlanners.tests.test_core.test_belief.test_belief_update_with_different_actions()[source]

Test that belief update behaves differently for different actions.

POMDPPlanners.tests.test_core.test_belief.test_belief_update_with_extreme_weights()[source]

Test belief update with extreme weight values.

POMDPPlanners.tests.test_core.test_belief.test_belief_update_with_resampling()[source]

Test belief update with resampling enabled.

POMDPPlanners.tests.test_core.test_belief.test_belief_update_with_tiger_pomdp()[source]

Test belief update with TigerPOMDP environment.

Test is_terminal_belief returns True when all particles are terminal.

Test is_terminal_belief with large mixed particle set.

Test is_terminal_belief with large number of particles.

Test is_terminal_belief returns False when some particles are terminal.

Test is_terminal_belief returns False when no particles are terminal.

Test is_terminal_belief with single non-terminal particle.

Test multiple inplace_updates accumulate correctly.

POMDPPlanners.tests.test_core.test_belief.test_weighted_particle_belief_state_update_mutable_default_arguments()[source]

Test that WeightedParticleBeliefStateUpdate doesn’t have mutable default argument issues.

Purpose: Validates that multiple empty belief instances don’t share references to the same list objects

Given: Multiple WeightedParticleBeliefStateUpdate instances created with default empty initialization When: Instances are created and their particle/weight lists are checked for reference sharing Then: Each instance has its own separate empty lists, preventing mutable default argument issues

Test type: unit

POMDPPlanners.tests.test_core.test_belief.test_weighted_particle_belief_state_update_sample_basic()[source]

Test basic sampling functionality.

POMDPPlanners.tests.test_core.test_belief.test_weighted_particle_belief_state_update_sample_empty_belief_raises_error()[source]

Test that sampling from empty belief raises ValueError.

POMDPPlanners.tests.test_core.test_belief.test_weighted_particle_belief_state_update_sample_empty_particles_raises_error()[source]

Test that creating belief with mismatched particles and weights raises ValueError.
